# AGENTS_MD_IS_ORIENTATION_SURFACE

**Tier:** Shipped default — generated by `dome init` for every new vault; refreshed by `dome doctor --repair`. Vault owners can edit the user-prose section freely; the templated sections regenerate on demand.

**Statement:** The vault root carries an `AGENTS.md` file that is the canonical orientation surface for agentic harnesses interacting with the vault. The file teaches the agent the vault's conventions, page types, named workflows, invariant set, and any vault-owner-authored prose (delimited via HTML comments). A Claude-Code-compatible `CLAUDE.md` shim at vault root points at `AGENTS.md` so Claude Code's auto-loading mechanism picks up the orientation transparently.

`AGENTS.md`'s contents are split into **templated sections** (regenerated by `dome init` and `dome doctor --repair` from the vault's current `.dome/config.yaml`, enabled invariant set, declared page types, and shipped/active workflow set) and a **user-prose section** (delimited by `<!-- BEGIN user-prose --> ... <!-- END user-prose -->` comments) that the vault owner edits freely. The user-prose section is preserved verbatim across all `--repair` runs.

**Why:** Per [[VISION]] §"Two surface patterns" and [[wiki/specs/harnesses]] §"The compiler-boundary contract", `AGENTS.md` is one of the four load-bearing surfaces of the compiler boundary — the orientation surface for agentic harnesses. Without `AGENTS.md` (or with a stale one), an agent arriving at a Dome vault would have no way to know its conventions, named operations, or active invariants short of reading the SDK source — and most agents wouldn't. `AGENTS.md` is what makes "the agent arrives oriented" work without each harness implementing per-vault discovery logic.

Why this is shipped-default rather than opt-in: the cost of an absent `AGENTS.md` (silent agent confusion: missed page-type-by-directory, missed wikilink-fullpath convention, missed workflow invocations) is high and silent; the cost of a present `AGENTS.md` is a few KB of markdown at vault root. The asymmetry is decisive.

**Structural enforcement:**

- `dome init` writes `AGENTS.md` (templated sections + an empty user-prose section with the delimiters in place) and `CLAUDE.md` (the shim, content `See AGENTS.md.`) at vault root. The initial git commit captures both.
- `dome doctor --repair` regenerates the templated sections of `AGENTS.md` from the current vault state (config, enabled invariants, declared page types, shipped/active workflows). The user-prose section is parsed via the delimiter comments and re-emitted verbatim. If the file is missing entirely, `--repair` recreates it (treating the user-prose section as empty).
- `dome doctor` (without `--repair`) reports drift: `AGENTS.md` missing → violation; templated sections out of sync with current config → violation; `CLAUDE.md` shim missing or pointing at the wrong file → violation.

**Counter-example (what this prevents):** A user runs `dome init` on a new vault. Six months later, they enable `PAGE_CREATION_REQUIRES_RECURRENCE` in `.dome/config.yaml`. Without this invariant's `--repair` mechanism, the `AGENTS.md` orientation would still say the invariant is disabled. The agent reads the stale orientation and proposes new pages without recurrence rationale; `writeDocument` refuses; user confusion. With `--repair`, the templated sections regenerate from current config, the agent learns the invariant is enabled, and proposes pages with the required rationale.

**Test guarantee:** A regression test scaffolds a fresh vault with `dome init`, asserts `AGENTS.md` exists with the expected templated sections and an empty user-prose delimiter pair. A second test adds user-prose content, mutates `.dome/config.yaml` (enabling a new invariant), runs `dome doctor --repair`, asserts the templated sections updated and the user-prose section is preserved byte-for-byte. A third test deletes `AGENTS.md` entirely, runs `dome doctor` (without `--repair`), asserts a violation is emitted naming the missing file.

**Operational notes:**

- `AGENTS.md` is per-vault, not per-codebase. Two vaults on the same machine have independent `AGENTS.md` files reflecting their independent configurations.
- The file is vault-owned (committed to git, edited by the user); the SDK never clobbers the user-prose section. The templated sections are explicitly fenced off via the delimiter comments so the user knows what is and isn't safe to edit by hand.
- The `CLAUDE.md` shim exists because Claude Code's auto-load convention currently prefers `CLAUDE.md`; removable once `AGENTS.md` auto-load is universal across harnesses (see [[wiki/specs/cli]] §"`dome init`").

**Related:**
